[[Tai Sodje]] (son) | education = }} }} '''Bright Ojamohare Sodje''' (born 21 April 1966) is a retired [[dual-code rugby]] player, who played for [[rugby league]] clubs; the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity (Wildcats)]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], and the [[Sheffield Eagles (1984)|Sheffield Eagles]] in England as well as [[rugby union]] for [[Wakefield RFC]]. Sodje's brothers, [[Sam Sodje|Sam]], [[Efe Sodje|Efe]], [[Akpo Sodje|Akpo]], Steve, and his nephew, [[Onome Sodje|Onome]], have all played professional [[Association football|football]] in England. Bright was a winger with a reputation for scoring tries, and noted for his aeroplane style celebration. ==Financial Crisis at Wakefield Trinity Wildcats== In 2000, at the height of a financial crisis at Wakefield Trinity Wildcats, the contracts of all players aged over 24 were terminated during September 2000. The players affected were; [[Andy Fisher (rugby league)|Andy Fisher]], [[Bobbie Goulding]], [[Warren Jowitt]], [[Tony Kemp (rugby league)|Tony Kemp]] (player-coach), [[Steve McNamara]], [[Francis Maloney (rugby league)|Francis Maloney]], [[Martin Masella]], [[Steve Prescott]], Bright Sodje, [[Francis Stephenson]] and [[Glen Tomlinson]].{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/1295270.stm|title=Goodway calls for aid package|publisher=BBC Sport|date=2001-04-24|access-date=2010-01-01}} ==Personal life== In September 2017, Bright was sentenced to 21 months in prison for [[fraud]], having – with his brothers Efe and Stephen – [[Embezzlement|siphoned off money]] from a charity, the Sodje Sports Foundation.{{cite news |title=Efe, Bright and Stephen Sodje jailed for fraud last September, court reveals |url=https://www.skysports.com/football/news/11095/11613628/efe-bright-and-stephen-sodje-jailed-for-fraud-last-september-court-reveals |accessdate=21 January 2019 |publisher=Sky News |date=21 January 2019}} His son, [[Tai Sodje|Tai]], is a professional footballer, and currently plays for the academy team of [[Manchester City F.C.|Manchester City]].{{cite web|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/football/football-news/man-city-tai-sodje-interview-20231748|title='I think I've opened up to different ways of playing': Man City academy striker Tai Sodje is firing his way to goals target|first=Simon|last=Bajkowski|date=23 March 2021|accessdate=18 February 2023|website=manchestereveningnews.co.uk}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External
